Auth2FA.title: 2FA Authenticator
Auth2FA.description: Generate time-based one-time passwords (TOTP) securely in your browser.
Auth2FA.invalidKey: Invalid Secret Key. Please check and try again.
Auth2FA.privacyNotice: Your secret keys remain on your device. Codes are generated locally.

Tool.title: Text Edit Tools
Tool.description: Manipulate, format, and transform your text quickly and securely in the browser.

CheckIP.title: IP Geolocation
CheckIP.description: Check details and location of your IP address.

CheckUID.title: Check UID Live/Die
CheckUID.description: Bulk check Facebook UIDs to see if they are active (Live) or disabled (Die).
CheckUID.noUids: No valid Facebook UIDs found in the input.

GetUID.title: Get UID Facebook
GetUID.description: Extract Facebook User/Page IDs (UID) from URLs or usernames in bulk.
GetUID.noUrls: Please enter URLs or usernames to get UIDs.

Notepad.title: Notepad Online
Notepad.description: Save your notes securely. Share via URL.

FAQ.title: Frequently Asked Questions
FAQ.description: Everything you need to know about how the 2FA Tools platform works, security measures, and privacy policies.
FAQ.footer: Still have questions? The source code is open for inspection.
FAQ.q1: Are my 2FA secret keys sent to the server?
FAQ.a1: No. All TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password) generation happens entirely in your browser using client-side JavaScript. Your secret keys never leave your device.
FAQ.q2: Is the text manipulation tool secure for sensitive data?
FAQ.a2: Yes. The text editor tool operates 100% locally in your browser. None of the text you paste or modify is sent to our servers or stored anywhere.
FAQ.a3: Notepad entries are securely stored in a local SQLite database if you are self-hosting this platform. Only people with the unique, randomly generated URL can access a specific note.
FAQ.q4: How does the 2FA Authenticator generate codes?
FAQ.a4: It uses the standard HMAC-based algorithm (RFC 6238) to generate a 6-digit code based on the current time and the secret key you provide. This is exactly how apps like Google Authenticator and Authy work.
FAQ.q5: How does the Facebook UID Checker work?
FAQ.a5: The UID Checker queries Facebook's public profile picture endpoint. If a valid picture is returned, the UID is marked as 'Live'. If an error or silhouette is returned, it is marked as 'Die' (disabled or non-existent).
FAQ.q6: How does the IP Checker get my location?
FAQ.a6: We use a combination of server-side headers and a third-party IP geolocation API to accurately determine your country, city, ISP, and approximate coordinates.
FAQ.q7: Does this work on mobile devices?
FAQ.a7: Absolutely. The entire platform is built with a responsive, mobile-first design, ensuring all tools work seamlessly on smartphones and tablets.
FAQ.a8: Yes! This project is designed to be easily self-hosted using Docker. Simply pull the repository, run the docker-compose command, and you'll have your own private instance running in seconds.
FAQ.q9: Does this platform have ads or tracking?
FAQ.a9: No. This clone was built specifically to provide a clean, ad-free, and blazing-fast experience without any intrusive tracking scripts.

StringSplitTool.title: String & Line Splitter
StringSplitTool.desc: Extract columns by delimiter or split text into chunks.

FilterTool.title: Filter Strings
FilterTool.desc: Keep or remove lines containing specific keywords.

DuplicateFilterTool.title: Remove Duplicates
DuplicateFilterTool.desc: Filter out duplicate lines from your text.

ReverseTextTool.title: Reverse Text
ReverseTextTool.desc: Reverse words, characters, or lines in your text.

MergeTool.title: Merge Lines & Strings
MergeTool.desc: Combine two lists line-by-line with a separator.

HtmlExtractorTool.title: HTML Extractor
HtmlExtractorTool.desc: Extract Image URLs or Links from HTML code.

CookieTool.title: Cookie Tools
CookieTool.desc: Manage Facebook Cookies: extract UID, tokens, sort, and remove dead cookies.
CookieTool.placeholder: Paste Cookie here (1 per line)\nExample:\ndatr=xxx; c_user=100001234567; xs=xxx; fr=xxx
CookieTool.tip1: Paste Cookie in Netscape or Header format (1 per line)
CookieTool.tip2: System will find c_user=UID in each line automatically
CookieTool.tip3: 'Remove Dead Cookies' removes lines without c_user

UidToYearTool.title: UID -> Created Year
UidToYearTool.desc: Estimate Facebook account creation year from UID (Supports raw UID or Cookie).
UidToYearTool.placeholder:
Paste UID (1 per line) or Cookie

Example:
100001234567890
100009876543210
c_user=100015678901234; xs=xxx
UidToYearTool.noteDesc: Years are estimated based on Facebook UID ranges. Results may vary by ±1 year. Accounts before 2009 have short UIDs (8-10 digits), while newer accounts start with 100xxx.

PomodoroTool.breakNotifBody: Great job! Take a well-deserved rest.
PomodoroTool.workNotifBody: Let's get back to being productive!
